syntax.syntax_item: script statements, function localFunction(...), statements, end

description.p: end closes a function definition. The legacy endfunction keyword is not supported.
Function files may end at end-of-file for simple function bodies, but explicit end is required to close nested functions, local functions, and block constructs unambiguously.
Function files can contain local functions after the main function. Script files can contain executable script statements followed by trailing local functions.

Local functions in scripts must be placed after all script statements. Nelson rejects script statements after local functions and rejects local functions declared inside open statement contexts such as if, for, while, switch, and try.

Nested functions are supported inside parent function bodies. Nested functions can read and update variables from their parent function workspace, and handles to nested functions keep their captured state.

example_item.example_item_description: Nested function sharing a parent variable.
